generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building actuator for lighting control systems integrated with BCS, enabling bidirectional control of adaptive lighting mechanisms. Device configurations including tenant IDs and endpoint addresses are embedded as metadata within BIM models for automated deployment via Eclipse Ditto."
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalBuildingSystem
]

instance HVACController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building control system actuator for HVAC units, supporting real-time temperature and humidity regulation. Control logic and device endpoints are modeled within BIM environments for seamless integration with Ditto middleware."
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalBuildingSystem
]

instance AdaptiveShadingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Building actuator for automated shading systems that adjust based on occupancy and solar exposure data. Device configurations are embedded in BIM models for automated commissioning through Eclipse Ditto."
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalBuildingSystem
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT temperature sensor deployed within building infrastructure to capture real-time operational data. Integrated with BCS and configured via Eclipse Hono for automatic data extraction from BIM metadata during deployment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalBuildingSystem
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT humidity sensor providing real-time environmental monitoring data. Sensor configurations including data formats and sampling rates are embedded as metadata within BIM models for automated ingestion via Hono."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalBuildingSystem
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT occupancy sensor detecting real-time building usage patterns. Device metadata including tenant IDs and endpoints are automatically extracted from BIM during deployment for Ditto integration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalBuildingSystem
]

instance EnergyMonitoringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor for real-time energy consumption monitoring across building systems. Post-occupancy data is used for iterative refinement of control policies as demonstrated in BOREALIS project."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn PhysicalBuildingSystem
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyMonitoringSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMBuildingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Centralized BIM model serving as the static semantic backbone for the digital twin, containing geometric, topological, and device configuration metadata. Enriched with runtime semantics for operational monitoring and control."
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor, OccupancySensor, EnergyMonitoringSensor,
                 LightingActuator, HVACController, AdaptiveShadingActuator
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

instance OperationalDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Runtime operational database storing real-time sensor readings, control signals, and historical building performance data. Automatically populated via Eclipse Hono data ingestion pipeline."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnergyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnalyticsEngine
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

instance ControlPolicyModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM-embedded model containing adaptive control policies for lighting, HVAC, and shading systems. Supports iterative refinement through reinforcement learning as demonstrated in BOREALIS project."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

instance EnergyPerformanceSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulation model integrated with BIM for predictive energy performance analysis. Used for optimizing control policies based on post-occupancy sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineVisualizer
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data ingestion middleware for IoT devices, automatically extracting sensor metadata from BIM models during deployment and streaming data to the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service-oriented platform for bidirectional communication between physical devices and digital twin, managing device twins and executing runtime control logic."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveControlService, DeviceManagement, ControlExecution
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance BIMValidationTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Tool for validating BIM model semantic consistency before automated deployment, ensuring proper device configuration metadata is embedded."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelValidation
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance DittoConfigurator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MDE-based tool for automated generation of Ditto device configurations from BIM model metadata, enabling scalable deployment of device twins."
    DTDFVocab:enables ConfigurationGeneration, DeviceManagement
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualizer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization platform for interactive monitoring of building operations, providing immersive dashboards for facility managers and engineers."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationDashboardService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance AnalyticsEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data processing engine for predictive analytics including energy consumption trends and equipment failure risk assessment."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time operational monitoring of building systems through sensor data processing and visualization."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, OccupancyPatternInsight
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance AdaptiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service deploying and managing adaptive control policies for lighting, HVAC, and shading systems based on real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationAction, ComfortAdjustmentAction
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing operational data to predict equipment failures and recommend maintenance actions."
    DTDFVocab:provides EquipmentFailureAlert, MaintenanceRecommendation
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance VisualizationDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing 3D immersive visualization of building operations through Unreal Engine interface."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingVisualizationInsight, StakeholderReport
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance ModelValidation : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for validating BIM model consistency and ensuring proper device configuration metadata is embedded."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance ConfigurationGeneration : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for automated generation of device configurations from BIM model metadata."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance DeviceManagement : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for managing device twins and their configurations within Eclipse Ditto."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance ControlExecution : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for executing runtime control logic based on control policies."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational performance metrics including energy consumption, system efficiency, and operational anomalies detected through sensor data analysis."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

instance OccupancyPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of building occupancy patterns to optimize space utilization and energy management based on sensor data trends."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of control policies to optimize energy consumption based on predictive analytics and real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

instance ComfortAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of HVAC and lighting systems to maintain optimal occupant comfort conditions based on real-time occupancy data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

instance EquipmentFailureAlert : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive alerts indicating potential equipment failures based on degradation patterns detected in sensor data streams."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested maintenance actions for building systems based on predictive maintenance analysis, prioritized by criticality and cost-effectiveness."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

instance BuildingVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D immersive visualization of building operations including real-time sensor data representation and system performance indicators."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

instance StakeholderReport : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Customizable reports for different stakeholders including tenant comfort metrics, energy performance summaries, and operational efficiency indicators."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment
]

// C1: Environment
instance BuildingInfrastructureEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "The physical and virtual environment encompassing the building infrastructure and its digital twin representation."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment PhysicalBuildingSystem, DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance PhysicalBuildingSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical building components including sensors and actuators."
]

instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital twin components including middleware, services, and visualization tools."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Virtual-to-physical interaction is achieved through **runtime control logic** embedded in the DT, which generates **actuation commands** for physical systems via the **Eclipse Ditto middleware**. The framework enables **bidirectional communication** between the DT and the BCS, allowing the DT to **adjust lighting, HVAC, or other adaptive systems** based on real-time analytics. The **TwinLight project** introduces a **layered system architecture** where BIM models are coupled with middleware for **automated control logic deployment**, ensuring that **control signals** (e.g., lighting adjustments) are **directly fed back** to physical actuators. The **BOREALIS project** further extends this by using **reinforcement learning** to optimize control policies, demonstrating the DT’s ability to **dynamically adjust physical systems** based on virtual simulations."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale is **real-time and event-driven**, with **millisecond-to-second granularity** for critical control operations (e.g., lighting adjustments) and **minute-to-hour intervals** for broader analytics (e.g., energy consumption trends). The **Eclipse Hono and Ditto middleware** ensure **low-latency data exchange**, enabling **sub-second synchronization** between physical sensors and the DT. The **BOREALIS project** further emphasizes **iterative optimization cycles**, where control policies are refined **hourly to daily** based on post-occupancy data. The framework supports **asynchronous event processing** for non-critical updates (e.g., occupancy changes) while maintaining **strict real-time constraints** for control actions."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented architecture** with **Eclipse Ditto as the central hub**, enabling **bidirectional communication** between physical systems and the DT. The constellation includes:
   - **BIM models** as the **semantic foundation**, enriched with **runtime metadata**.
   - **Eclipse Hono** for **sensor data ingestion** and **device authentication**.
   - **Unreal Engine 5** for **visualization and user interaction**.
   - **Custom services** (e.g., energy optimization, predictive maintenance) integrated via **modular extension points**.
   The framework supports **heterogeneous data sources** (e.g., IoT sensors, BCS protocols) and **cross-platform interoperability**, ensuring seamless integration with **existing AECO workflows**. The **modular design** allows **incremental deployment** of services, enabling **scalable, adaptive DT ecosystems**."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT operates across **all life cycle stages** of the building, from **design (as-designed)** to **construction (as-manufactured)** and **operation (as-operated)**. During **design**, the DT leverages **BIM models** for **clash detection and performance simulation**. In **construction**, it supports **automated device commissioning** via **Eclipse Hono**. During **operation**, the DT enables **real-time monitoring, control, and adaptive optimization** using **runtime sensor data**. The framework also accommodates **refurbishment and adaptation phases**, where **BIM models are updated** to reflect changes in the physical infrastructure, ensuring **continuous synchronization** between the DT and the PT. The **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** is used to evaluate the DT’s fidelity across these stages."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
   - **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which quantifies the **alignment between the DT and the physical twin (PT)** by evaluating **data consistency, synchronization latency, and control accuracy**.
   - **Automated validation** of BIM models to detect **semantic inconsistencies** (e.g., missing device configurations).
   - **Real-time error detection** via **Eclipse Ditto’s telemetry** for **anomaly monitoring** in sensor/actuator data.
   - **Post-occupancy data analysis** (as in **BOREALIS**) to **validate control policies** and **adjust models** iteratively.
   The framework accounts for **uncertainty in sensor data** and **model drift** (as discussed in Abbasi et al.) by incorporating **adaptive learning mechanisms** into the DT’s control logic."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT employs **Eclipse Ditto’s MQTT-based protocol** for **low-latency, bidirectional communication** between physical systems and the DT. Data is transmitted via **Eclipse Hono’s secure ingestion layer**, which handles **authentication, device management, and data normalization**. The framework supports **TCP/IP-based connections** for **real-time synchronization**, with **customizable QoS levels** (e.g., at-least-once delivery for critical control signals). The **Unreal Engine 5 integration** uses **RESTful APIs** for **visualization updates**, ensuring **seamless interaction** between the DT’s virtual and physical components."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is **hosted in a cloud-native environment**, leveraging **Eclipse Ditto’s Kubernetes-based deployment** for **scalability and fault tolerance**. The framework supports **on-premise or hybrid cloud hosting**, with **containerized microservices** (e.g., Ditto, Hono) ensuring **modular scalability**. The **Unreal Engine 5 runtime** is deployed as a **standalone client application** for **interactive visualization**, while **BIM models** are stored in **centralized repositories** (e.g., BIMserver) for **version control**. The framework adheres to **open-source licensing** (e.g., Eclipse Public License) to minimize **vendor lock-in** and enable **customizable deployment architectures**."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The DT facilitates **horizontal integration** with **external information systems** via:
   - **Standardized APIs** (e.g., REST, MQTT) for **interoperability** with **ERP, CMMS, or IoT platforms**.
   - **BIM integration** with **industry standards** (e.g., IFC, COBie) for **seamless data exchange** across AECO workflows.
   - **Modular service plugins** that enable **integration with third-party analytics** (e.g., AI-driven optimization tools).
   The framework supports **event-driven architectures** (e.g., via **Eclipse Ditto’s event bus**) to **sync with external systems** (e.g., **building management systems, energy grids**) without **direct coupling**. This ensures **flexible, scalable connectivity** with **heterogeneous IT landscapes**."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "Data ownership is **shared between the building owner and DT service provider**, with **clear contractual agreements** defining **access rights and usage policies**. The framework adheres to **GDPR and AECO-specific privacy regulations**, ensuring:
   - **Encrypted data transmission** (e.g., TLS for MQTT) to protect **sensor and operational data**.
   - **Role-based access control (RBAC)** via **Eclipse Ditto**, restricting **data exposure** to authorized stakeholders (e.g., facility managers, engineers).
   - **Anonymization of tenant-specific data** where required to comply with **privacy laws**.
   The framework does not specify **explicit data ownership models** beyond **collaborative governance**, leaving **customization** to project-specific agreements."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **multiple industry standards** to ensure **interoperability and scalability**:
   - **Building Information Modeling (BIM)**: Uses **IFC (Industry Foundation Classes)** and **COBie (Construction Operations Building Information Exchange)** for **semantic consistency**.
   - **Eclipse Ditto**: Complies with **OPC UA** and **MQTT** for **device connectivity**.
   - **Eclipse Hono**: Follows **IoT security standards** (e.g., **X.509 certificates, JWT authentication**) for **secure data ingestion**.
   - **Unreal Engine 5**: Supports **standardized 3D visualization protocols** (e.g., **glTF, USDZ**) for **cross-platform compatibility**.
   The framework also integrates **modular extension points** to **comply with future AECO standards** (e.g., **BIM 4D/5D for time/cost integration**)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
   - **Eclipse Ditto’s built-in security** (e.g., **TLS 1.3, OAuth 2.0, JWT tokens**) for **authenticated communication**.
   - **Eclipse Hono’s device authentication** (e.g., **X.509 certificates**) to prevent **unauthorized access** to physical systems.
   - **Role-based access control (RBAC)** to restrict **DT manipulation** to authorized personnel (e.g., **facility managers, engineers**).
   - **Fail-safe mechanisms** in control logic to **prevent accidental actuation** (e.g., **emergency stop overrides**).
   The framework does **not explicitly address remote emergency operations**, but **control policies are designed to prioritize safety** (e.g., **fallback to manual modes** in case of DT failures). **Cybersecurity risks** (e.g., **DT hijacking**) are mitigated via **network segmentation** and **audit logging** in Ditto/Hono."
]
